annotations.label: Linear representation
annotations.note: `Representation k G V` is the abbreviation `G →* V →ₗ[k] V`, exactly the linear representation in the article.

annotations.label: Representation space

anchor.snippet: is called a representation space of

annotations.note: The carrier `V` of a `Representation k G V` is the representation space.

annotations.label: Degree of a representation

anchor.snippet: The degree of a representation is the dimension

annotations.note: There is no specific `Representation.degree`, but the degree is just `Module.finrank k V` (used in `FDRep.char_one`).

annotations.label: Trivial representation

anchor.snippet: The trivial representation is given by

annotations.note: `Representation.trivial k G V := 1` is exactly the trivial representation.

annotations.label: Degree-one representation as root of unity
annotations.note: No dedicated Mathlib decl characterizes degree-one representations of a finite group as roots of unity.

annotations.label: Linear representation of degree two
annotations.note: This Wikipedia example of a specific 2-dimensional representation is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Permutation representation

anchor.snippet: The permutation representation is the group homomorphism

annotations.note: `Representation.ofMulAction k G H` is the permutation representation on `H →₀ k` from a `G`-action on `H`.

annotations.label: Permutation representation example
annotations.note: Concrete example computations of permutation representations are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Left-regular representation

anchor.snippet: The left-regular representation is a special case of the permutation representation

annotations.note: `Representation.leftRegular k G := ofMulAction k G G` is precisely the left-regular representation.

annotations.label: Right-regular representation

anchor.snippet: The right-regular representation is defined on the same vector space

annotations.note: Mathlib has `leftRegular` but no separately named right-regular representation of an abstract finite group.

annotations.label: Left- and right-regular are isomorphic
annotations.note: No isomorphism between left and right regular representations is stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Criterion for isomorphism to left-regular

anchor.snippet: is isomorphic to the left-regular representation if and only if there exists

annotations.note: Such a criterion (cyclic vector for left-regular representation) is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Left- and right-regular representation example
annotations.note: No worked-out example of regular representations for a concrete group exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Representations correspond to group-algebra modules

anchor.section: Representations, modules and the convolution algebra
anchor.snippet: homomorphisms of representations are in bijective correspondence with group algebra homomorphisms

annotations.note: `equivLinearMapAsModule` gives the linear equivalence between intertwiners of representations and `k[G]`-linear maps between their `asModule`s.

annotations.label: Convolution algebra

anchor.section: Representations, modules and the convolution algebra

annotations.note: The group/convolution algebra `k[G]` is `MonoidAlgebra k G`.

annotations.label: Involution on the convolution algebra

anchor.section: Representations, modules and the convolution algebra

annotations.note: The involution `f*(s) = conj f(s⁻¹)` on the group algebra of a finite group is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fourier transform on a group

anchor.section: Representations, modules and the convolution algebra

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks the Fourier transform for finite (or non-abelian) groups.

annotations.label: Equivariant (G-linear) map

anchor.snippet: Such a map is also called

annotations.note: `Representation.IntertwiningMap ρ σ` is exactly the bundled G-equivariant linear map.

annotations.label: Subrepresentation
annotations.note: `Subrepresentation ρ` is a `G`-stable submodule of the representation space.

annotations.label: Irreducible representation

anchor.snippet: The representation is called an irreducible representation

annotations.note: `Representation.IsIrreducible ρ := IsSimpleOrder (Subrepresentation ρ)` formalizes irreducibility.

annotations.label: Schur's lemma

anchor.snippet: Schur's lemma puts a strong constraint on maps between irreducible representations

annotations.note: An intertwining map between irreducibles is either zero or bijective; see also `FDRep.finrank_hom_simple_simple`.

annotations.label: Equivalent/isomorphic representations
annotations.note: `Representation.Equiv ρ σ` is a bijective intertwining map, i.e. an isomorphism of representations.

annotations.label: Faithful representation
annotations.note: No `Representation.IsFaithful`-style predicate exists in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Restriction of a representation
annotations.note: `Rep.res f M` restricts `M : Rep k G` along `f : H →* G`; underlying it is `Representation.comp`.

annotations.label: Number of irreducibles equals number of conjugacy classes

anchor.snippet: It can be proven that the number of irreducible representations

annotations.note: This counting theorem (irreducibles ↔ conjugacy classes) does not appear in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Semisimple / completely reducible representation

anchor.snippet: A representation is called semisimple or completely reducible

annotations.note: `IsSemisimpleRepresentation ρ` is `ComplementedLattice (Subrepresentation ρ)`.

annotations.label: Isotypic representation
annotations.note: No `Isotypic` predicate for representations exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isotype of a representation

anchor.snippet: is defined as the sum of all irreducible subrepresentations

annotations.note: The isotype/isotypic component is not separately defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unitary representation
annotations.note: There is no `Representation.IsUnitary` class in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unitary iff invariant inner product

anchor.snippet: A representation is unitary with respect to a given inner product if and only if

annotations.note: No such equivalence statement is in Mathlib (no unitary-representation API).

annotations.label: Dihedral group representation

anchor.snippet: be the dihedral group of order

annotations.note: No concrete dihedral group representation example is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dual (contragredient) representation

anchor.snippet: The dual representation or contragredient representation

annotations.note: `Representation.dual ρ` sends `g` to `f ↦ f ∘ ρ g⁻¹`, the contragredient action.

annotations.label: Direct sum of representations

anchor.snippet: The direct sum of these representations is a linear representation

annotations.note: `Representation.directSum` constructs the direct sum representation on `⨁ i, V i`.

annotations.label: Direct sum example

anchor.snippet: are the imaginary unit and the primitive cube root of unity

annotations.note: This specific example with i and the cube root of unity is not formalized.

annotations.label: Outer tensor product of representations

anchor.snippet: This representation is called outer tensor product

annotations.note: Mathlib has the same-group tensor product `tprod`, but the outer tensor product for two different groups is not given its own definition.

annotations.label: Outer tensor product example

anchor.snippet: We reexamine the example provided for the direct sum

annotations.note: This concrete example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tensor product of two representations of same group

anchor.snippet: which is also called tensor product of the given representations

annotations.note: `Representation.tprod ρ σ` is the diagonal tensor representation on `V ⊗ W`.

annotations.label: Irreducible representations of a direct product
annotations.note: The classification of irreducibles of `G × H` as outer tensor products is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symmetric square and alternating square

anchor.snippet: are called the symmetric square and the alternating square

annotations.note: No symmetric/alternating square of a representation is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Maschke's theorem
annotations.note: Every `k[G]`-submodule has a complement (Maschke's theorem) when `|G|` is invertible in `k`.

annotations.label: Compact group representations decompose into irreducibles

anchor.snippet: Every linear representation of a compact group over a field of characteristic zero is a direct sum

annotations.note: Decomposition of compact-group representations into irreducibles is not formalized.

annotations.label: Canonical decomposition

anchor.snippet: It is called the canonical decomposition

annotations.note: The canonical decomposition into isotypes is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Projection formula (finite groups)
annotations.note: Mathlib has `averageMap`, projecting onto invariants; the general character-twisted projection onto isotypes is not formalized.

annotations.label: Projection to trivial isotype

anchor.snippet: This proposition enables us to determine the isotype to the trivial subrepresentation

annotations.note: `isProj_averageMap` shows the group-averaging map projects onto the invariants (the trivial isotype).

annotations.label: Decomposition example with symmetric group on three elements

anchor.snippet: be the permutation groups in three elements

annotations.note: No worked S₃ decomposition example is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Character of a representation
annotations.note: `FDRep.character V g = trace (V.ρ g)`; `Representation.character` is also provided.

annotations.label: Character of a permutation representation

anchor.snippet: Characters of permutation representations are particularly easy to compute

annotations.note: The fixed-point formula for permutation characters is not stated.

annotations.label: Character is a class function

anchor.snippet: A crucial property of characters is the formula

annotations.note: `FDRep.char_conj` proves `χ(hgh⁻¹) = χ(g)`.

annotations.label: Class function (informal)
annotations.note: No `ClassFunction` predicate/structure exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Class function (formal)
annotations.note: There is no formal `ClassFunction` type in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inner product on class functions

anchor.snippet: An inner product can be defined on the set of all class functions

annotations.note: The pairing `⟨f,g⟩ = (1/|G|) Σ f(g) g(g)*` on class functions is not isolated as a named definition.

annotations.label: Orthonormal property of irreducible characters
annotations.note: `FDRep.char_orthonormal` gives orthonormality of irreducible characters over an algebraically closed field.

annotations.label: Number of irreducibles equals number of conjugacy classes (via characters)

anchor.snippet: It follows from the orthonormal property that the number of non-isomorphic irreducible representations

annotations.note: This corollary (cardinality of `Irr G` = number of conjugacy classes) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multiplicity of irreducible in a decomposition equals inner product of characters
annotations.note: Mathlib has `⟨χ_V,χ_W⟩ = dim Hom_G(V,W)`; the multiplicity-as-inner-product corollary follows by Schur but isn't separately stated.

annotations.label: Representations with same character are isomorphic

anchor.snippet: Two representations with the same character are isomorphic

annotations.note: Mathlib has `char_iso` (the forward direction) but not the converse `same character → isomorphic` for semisimple representations.

annotations.label: Irreducibility criterion via inner product of character
annotations.note: The criterion `⟨χ,χ⟩ = 1 ↔ irreducible` is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Irreducible occurs in regular representation with multiplicity equal to its degree
annotations.note: This statement about multiplicities in the regular representation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fourier inversion formula
annotations.note: No group-Fourier inversion formula is in Mathlib for finite groups.

annotations.label: Plancherel formula
annotations.note: The Plancherel formula for finite groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Abelian iff all irreducibles have degree one
annotations.note: Mathlib has the forward direction (abelian implies dim 1) but the equivalence is not stated.

annotations.label: Induced representation

anchor.snippet: is called induced by the representation

annotations.note: `Representation.ind φ ρ` is the `H`-representation induced from a `G`-representation `ρ` along `φ : G →* H`.

annotations.label: Alternative description of the induced representation

anchor.section: Alternative description of the induced representation

annotations.note: The alternative `H`-stable decomposition characterization is not given.

annotations.label: Existence and uniqueness of induced representation

anchor.snippet: Uniqueness and existence of the induced representation

annotations.note: Mathlib gives the induction–restriction adjunction (which packages the universal property), but no explicit existence/uniqueness theorem in the textbook form.

annotations.label: Transitivity of induction
annotations.note: No `Ind_K^G ∘ Ind_H^K ≅ Ind_H^G` lemma exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Extension lemma for induced representations

anchor.snippet: Then there exists a uniquely determined linear map

annotations.note: This extension lemma for maps from `Ind ρ` is not separately stated.

annotations.label: Induction on class functions
annotations.note: Induction of class functions is not formalized.

annotations.label: Induced of a character is the character of the induced

anchor.snippet: is the character of the induced representation

annotations.note: Not present: char(Ind ρ) = Ind(char ρ).

annotations.label: Frobenius identity for class-function induction
annotations.note: No Frobenius identity for induced class functions in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Formula for the induced character

anchor.snippet: The induced character is given by

annotations.note: The explicit summation formula for the induced character is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Frobenius reciprocity
annotations.note: The Hom-level adjunction `Hom(Ind A, B) ≃ Hom(A, Res B)` is formalized; the character-level Frobenius reciprocity identity is not.

annotations.label: Disjoint representations
annotations.note: No `Disjoint` predicate for representations is in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: is irreducible if and only if the following conditions are satisfied

annotations.note: Mackey's criterion is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Irreducibility of induced representation from normal subgroup

anchor.snippet: and not isomorphic to the conjugates

annotations.note: This corollary of Mackey's criterion is not formalized.

anchor.snippet: Then one of the following statements has to be valid

annotations.note: Not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Degree divides index for abelian normal subgroup
annotations.note: Itô's theorem on dim ρ | [G:A] is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classification of irreducibles of a semidirect product

anchor.section: Classification of representations of a semidirect product

annotations.note: Mackey-Wigner classification for semidirect products is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Representation ring
annotations.note: The representation ring R(G) (Grothendieck group of Rep G) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Virtual representations
annotations.note: No virtual representation class is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Virtual characters
annotations.note: No virtual character class exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Artin's induction theorem

anchor.snippet: Artin's induction theorem is the most elementary theorem

annotations.note: Artin's induction theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Brauer's induction theorem
annotations.note: Brauer's induction theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Elementary subgroup

anchor.snippet: a group H is elementary if there is some prime

annotations.note: The notion of `p`-elementary subgroup (for Brauer's theorem) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Real representation (complexification)
annotations.note: No `IsRealRepresentation` predicate exists.

annotations.label: Real-valued character but not real representation

anchor.snippet: But not every representation with a real-valued character is real

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Real iff invariant nondegenerate symmetric bilinear form

anchor.snippet: is real if and only if there exists a nondegenerate symmetric bilinear form

annotations.note: Not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complex-conjugate factors of restricted real representation

anchor.snippet: there are exactly two irreducible factors which are complex conjugate representations

annotations.note: Not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quaternionic representation
annotations.note: No `Quaternionic` representation predicate exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trichotomy: complex, real, quaternionic

anchor.snippet: is one and only one of the following

annotations.note: Frobenius-Schur trichotomy is not formalized.

annotations.label: Specht module
annotations.note: Specht modules are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cuspidal representations

anchor.snippet: new building blocks, known as cuspidal representations

annotations.note: Cuspidal representations are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Topological / compact group

anchor.snippet: A topological group is a group together with a topology

annotations.note: Mathlib's `IsTopologicalGroup` formalizes topological groups; `CompactSpace` handles compactness.

annotations.label: Linear representation of a compact group
annotations.note: `ContRepresentation R G V := G →* V →L[R] V` formalizes continuous (linear) representations.

annotations.label: Linear representation on a Banach space
annotations.note: Mathlib's `ContRepresentation` works for topological modules; specializing to a Banach space is just adding the appropriate instances.

annotations.note: Existence via `MeasureTheory.Measure.haarMeasure` and uniqueness via the cited lemma cover the Haar-measure theorem.

anchor.section: Existence and uniqueness of the Haar measure

annotations.note: The unitary left-regular representation on `L²(G, dμ)` for a compact group is not formalized.

anchor.snippet: The right-regular representation is then the unitary representation

annotations.note: The unitary right-regular representation of a compact group is not formalized.

annotations.note: No continuous dual representation defined for compact groups in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Every irreducible representation of a compact group is finite-dimensional and unitarizable
annotations.note: Not formalized.
